title: There's Still Hope For a Black Best Director Oscar Winner
text: Marcus Jones examines the ongoing absence of a Black Best Director Oscar winner while focusing on director Ryan Coogler's trajectory. Jones notes he has "pre-written the 'X Becomes First Black Filmmaker to Win the Oscar for Best Director' article multiple times" across various publications but has never published it through the Academy's 98-year history. While "Sinners" director Ryan Coogler was nominated, Paul Thomas Anderson ultimately won for "One Battle After Another." Jones states, "I am not at all of the opinion that Paul Thomas Anderson is undeserving of his Oscars for his work." Jones argues Coogler's impact extends beyond representation discussions. He's established collaborators like cinematographer Autumn Durald Arkapaw, who became "the first woman to win the Best Cinematography Oscar" for "Sinners." The article suggests Coogler's career mirrors Steven Spielberg's path—both assemble world-class teams and took decades to win Best Director despite earlier acclaim and success.
